referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 4/1
referenceDocuments.description: Modernise local/network ATM system capabilities

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 4/2
referenceDocuments.description: Improve the quality of the European Aviation Network Planning Processes; Improve coordination of ATM transition projects

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 4/3
referenceDocuments.description: Collaborative Traffic & Network Management; Transformation of DCB into a collaborative constraint management process; Ensure the management of an enhanced Demand Capacity Balancing process

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 4/4

referenceDocuments.description: Aim to provide on-time operations for and at airport platforms with the support of the Network Manager

referenceDocuments.description: On-time execution of schedules

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 5/2
referenceDocuments.description: Extend NOP planning function to include the monitoring and identification of potential improvement measures for better on time execution of the airline schedules

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 5/3
referenceDocuments.description: Support the operations at and around airport platforms with appropriate data

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 5/4
referenceDocuments.description: Enhanced exchange of information between NMOC and APOC

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 5/5
referenceDocuments.description: Inter-modal connectivity

referenceDocuments.description: Enhance crisis management and the EACCC

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 6/6
referenceDocuments.description: Resilience and upwards/downwards scalability and societal needs

referenceDocuments.description: Optimise and Digitalise (as appropriate) ATM/CNS infrastructure and services to support evolutions towards more efficient network operations and services

referenceDocuments.description: Optimised and effective use of the aeronautical radio frequencies

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 7/2
referenceDocuments.description: Robust and transparent Radar Transponder Code Function, including the SSR code allocation process

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 7/3
referenceDocuments.description: Monitoring and reporting of performance and real-time service status

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 7/4
referenceDocuments.description: Propose and facilitate modernisation and decarbonisation of the ATM/CNS infrastructure

referenceDocuments.description: Ensure the development and provision of common network support services

referenceDocuments.name: ATMMP
referenceDocuments.description: ATM Master Plan 2025

referenceDocuments.name: ATMMP SDO 1
referenceDocuments.description: Alerts for reduction of collision risks on taxiways and runways

referenceDocuments.name: ATMMP SDO DA 1.1
referenceDocuments.description: Adapt airport ground safety nets to extend conflicting ATC clearances (CATC) to the entire aerodrome movement area, to enlarge the set of conformance monitoring (CMAC) alerting functions and to provide integrated occupancy/conflict status of a runway.

referenceDocuments.name: ATMMP SDO 2
referenceDocuments.description: Optimising airport and TMA environmental footprint

referenceDocuments.name: ATMMP SDO DA 2.1
referenceDocuments.description: Adapt airport ground safety nets to extend conflicting ATC clearances (CATC) to the entire aerodrome movement area, to enlarge the set of conformance monitoring (CMAC) alerting functions and to provide integrated occupancy/conflict status of a runway.

referenceDocuments.name: ATMMP SDO DA 2.2
referenceDocuments.description: Implement solutions to better integrate large/very large airports and the network via an enhanced AOPs-NOP tactical, pre-tactical and strategic planning and AOP to AOP collaborative planning process.

referenceDocuments.name: ATMMP SDO DA 2.3
referenceDocuments.description: Implement environmental performance management at airports and solutions to reduce the airport impact on emissions (single engine taxiing, engine-off taxiing though use of sustainable taxiing vehicles).

referenceDocuments.name: ATMMP SDO DA 2.4
referenceDocuments.description: Implement capabilities to better manage arrival constraints between various extended arrival management (E-AMAN) units in cross-border environments and to better integrate the out-of-area inbound flights.

referenceDocuments.name: ATMMP SDO DA 2.5
referenceDocuments.description: Implement optimised descent operations using merge to point and advanced approach procedures (46) (i.e. second runway-aiming point (SRAP), increased second glide slope (ISGS), increased glide slope to a second runway aiming point (IGS-to-SRAP)), which aim to reduce the environmental impact (e.g. noise, fuel consumption, CO2 Who ANSP, AU, AU MIL,  AO, NM ANSP, AU  AO, NM ANSP, AU,  AO Where Airport, TMA,  En-route, Network Airport, Network Airport ANSP, AU Airport, TMA,  En-route, Network ANSP, AU, AU MIL, AO Airport, TMA,  En-route emissions) on the airport's neighbouring communities.

referenceDocuments.name: ATMMP SDO DA 2.6
referenceDocuments.description: Implement new capabilities to increase airport runway capacity both on arrivals and departures using wake turbulence separations based on static aircraft characteristics, required surveillance performance (RSP) and runway occupancy time (ROT) characterisation of the leader aircraft.

referenceDocuments.name: ATMMP SDO 3
referenceDocuments.description: Dynamic airspace configuration

referenceDocuments.name: ATMMP SDO DA 3.1
referenceDocuments.description: Implement higher levels of granularity and dynamicity in airspace configurations, adjusted to traffic demand and military needs for airspace reservations, enabling cross-border coordination between all civil and military actors.

referenceDocuments.name: ATMMP SDO DA 3.2
referenceDocuments.description: Implement mission trajectory and dynamic mobile areas (DMAs) of type 1 and type 2 using the improved operational air traffic flight plan (iOAT FPL) into dynamic airspace configuration processes in the medium- to short-term ATM planning phase to support military airspace requirements.

referenceDocuments.name: ATMMP SDO 4
referenceDocuments.description: Increased automation support

referenceDocuments.name: ATMMP SDO DA 4.1
referenceDocuments.description: Implement sector team configurations, which in specific airspace configurations include the combination of one planning ATCO with two tactical/executive ATCOs in an en-route/eTMA environment.

referenceDocuments.name: ATMMP SDO DA 4.2
referenceDocuments.description: Implement automatic speech recognition (ASR), user profile management system (UPMS) and attention guidance (AG) to provide a higher automation environment to support the ATCO role.

referenceDocuments.name: ATMMP SDO 5
referenceDocuments.description: Transformation to trajectory-based operations (TBO)

referenceDocuments.name: ATMMP SDO DA 5.1
referenceDocuments.description: Implement enhanced conflict detection and resolution (CD&R) support tools by using aircraft-derived data (i.e. extended projected profile (EPP)) supported by the full implementation of ATS-B2 and high-resolution wind models.

referenceDocuments.name: ATMMP SDO DA 5.2
referenceDocuments.description: Implement multi-element clearances using controller pilot data link communications (CPDLC) with lateral and vertical data link clearances and increased ground automation tools (e.g. CD&R tools) and trajectory prediction supporting the earlier detection and resolution of potential conflicts.

referenceDocuments.name: ATMMP SDO DA 5.3
referenceDocuments.description: Implement a dynamic route availability document (RAD) to allow the dynamic management of restrictions based on traffic evolutions, better integration of letters of agreement (LoAs) between ATC centres and NM, and the provision of preliminary flight plans by airspace users. This will feed dynamic network constraints publications initiated the day before operations, to optimise the environmental performance of the network.

referenceDocuments.name: ATMMP SDO DA 5.4
referenceDocuments.description: Implement airspace user capabilities to provide, through the userdriven prioritisation process (UDPP), their preferences and priorities and influence ATFM arrival regulations.

referenceDocuments.name: ATMMP SDO DA 5.5
referenceDocuments.description: Implement interaction tools supporting the full integration of the flight operations centre (FOC) into the ATM network process, and the flight delay criticality concept, to better integrate airspace user priorities in flow management decisions.

referenceDocuments.name: ATMMP SDO DA 5.6
referenceDocuments.description: Exploit new FF-ICE/R1 trajectory services beyond the CP1 services (which are just the filing and trial services) to improve the completeness and accuracy of traffic load calculation and advanced network performance capabilities.

referenceDocuments.name: ATMMP SDO DA 5.7
referenceDocuments.description: Implement seamless ATC–ATC coordination and sharing with NM of the ATC–ATC exchanges (48), encompassing more complex coordination dialogues implying negotiation between controllers across ACC boundaries.

referenceDocuments.name: ATMMP SDO 6
referenceDocuments.description: Virtualisation of operations

referenceDocuments.name: ATMMP SDO DA 6.1
referenceDocuments.description: Implement virtual centres to enable decoupling of the ATM data service provider (ADSP) and ATSU through service interfaces that support new ways of dynamic ATS delegation (e.g. contingency delegation, night delegation (scheduled), fixed time delegation (scheduled), or 'on-demand').

referenceDocuments.name: ATMMP SDO DA 6.2
referenceDocuments.description: Implement multiple remote tower module (MRTM) flexible and dynamic allocation of different MRTMs accommodated within a remote tower centre (RTC) that allows the ATCO to maintain situational awareness for two or more small airports. It includes the implementation of a low-cost surveillance service for supporting remote tower operations.

referenceDocuments.name: ATMMP SDO 7
referenceDocuments.description: Transition towards high performance of air–ground connectivity (multilink)

referenceDocuments.name: ATMMP SDO DA 7.1
referenceDocuments.description: Implement future air–ground communications network infrastructure, which supports multilink capability and complete mobility between different data links.

referenceDocuments.name: ATMMP SDO DA 7.2
referenceDocuments.description: Implement SatCom class B, which enables data and voice communication services using existing satellite technology systems in oceanic, remote, polar and progressively continental airspace.

referenceDocuments.name: ATMMP SDO DA 7.3
referenceDocuments.description: Implement VDL-M2 successor (e.g. terrestrial data link system L-band-digital aeronautical communication system (LDACS), data link for ATM and AOC operations over commercial communication systems (hyper-connected ATM), and satellite communications for both continental and remote/oceanic regions.

referenceDocuments.name: ATMMP SDO 8
referenceDocuments.description: Service-oriented delivery model (data-driven and cloud-based)

referenceDocuments.name: ATMMP SDO DA 8.1
referenceDocuments.description: Implement the new service-oriented delivery model (data-driven and cloud-based) covering all phases of flight and enabling: open ATM patterns enabling integration of components provided by various system providers to facilitate multi-vendor solutions using open platforms and interfaces; decoupling of service and infrastructure layers through cloud computing (including the various system components); a cloud-native architecture of components with standardised and open interfaces that can be deployed on commodity cloud technologies.

referenceDocuments.name: ATMMP SDO 9
referenceDocuments.description: CNS optimisation, modernisation and resilience

referenceDocuments.name: ATMMP SDO DA 9.1
referenceDocuments.description: Implement a secured surveillance functionality that enables detection and, when possible, mitigation of security threats that could affect the surveillance chain.

referenceDocuments.name: ATMMP SDO DA 9.2
referenceDocuments.description: Implement minimum operational network (MON).

referenceDocuments.name: ATMMP SDO DA 9.3
referenceDocuments.description: Rationalise instrument landing systems (ILS) and implement efficiency measures/methods for more cost-effective maintenance of ILS, providing a link between ICAO Doc. 8071 and national CNS provision.

referenceDocuments.name: ATMMP SDO DA 9.4
referenceDocuments.description: Optimise surveillance, leveraging terrestrial and space-based information.

referenceDocuments.name: ATMMP SDO 10
referenceDocuments.description: Enable innovative air mobility (IAM) and drone operations

referenceDocuments.name: ATMMP SDO DA 10.1
referenceDocuments.description: Implement system support and procedures to integrate instrument flight rules (IFR) RPAS and IAM in airspaces A to C, which are required to have detect and avoid (DAA) systems that perform at least as well as TCAS II (traffic alert and collision avoidance system) and see and avoid.

referenceDocuments.name: ATMMP SDO DA 10.2
referenceDocuments.description: Implement foundational (U1) and initial (U2) U-space services as established by the regulatory framework for U-space (Commission Implementing Regulation (EU) 2021/664).

referenceDocuments.name: ATMMP SDO DA 10.3
referenceDocuments.description: Implement a common ATM–U-space interface and dynamic airspace reconfiguration service to help ATC actors in charge of airspace reconfigurations to increase safety, keeping crewed and uncrewed aircraft segregated within the designated U-space airspace.

referenceDocuments.name: ATMMP SDO DA 10.4
referenceDocuments.description: Implement simultaneous non-interfering (SNI) operations (e.g. parallel or convergent point-in-space (PinS) procedures) and capabilities (i.e. GNSS and the RNP navigation specification) allowing airspace users (e.g. rotorcraft, VTOL-capable aircraft, etc.) to operate to and from airports, vertiports and TMAs without conflicting with other traffic or requiring runway slots.

referenceDocuments.name: Commission Implementing Regulation (EU) 2021/116
referenceDocuments.description: SES Common Project One (CP1) - Initial measures of common projects

referenceDocuments.name: Commission Implementing Regulation (EU) 2019/123
referenceDocuments.description: Detailed rules for the implementation of air traffic management (ATM) network functions (Network Functions Implementing Rule)

referenceDocuments.name: Commission Implementing Regulation (EU) 2013/409
referenceDocuments.description: Implementing rules for the recovery actions referred to in Article 14 of Regulation (EC) No 549/2004

referenceDocuments.name: Commission Implementing Regulation (EU) 2019/317
referenceDocuments.description: Performance and Charging Scheme in the Single European Sky

referenceDocuments.name: Commission Implementing Regulation (EU) 2018/1048
referenceDocuments.description: Airspace usage requirements and operating procedures concerning Performance-Based Navigation

referenceDocuments.name: Regulation (EU) 2024/2803 of the European Parliament and of the Council
referenceDocuments.description: Implementation of the Single European Sky (recast)

referenceDocuments.name: Directive (EU) 2019/882 — European Accessibility Act
referenceDocuments.description: Accessibility requirements for products and services, including websites and mobile applications used by the public.

referenceDocuments.name: Directive (EU) 2016/2102 — Web Accessibility Directive
referenceDocuments.description: Accessibility of the websites and mobile applications of public sector bodies.

referenceDocuments.name: Regulation (EU) 2016/679 — General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR)
referenceDocuments.description: Protection of natural persons with regard to the processing of personal data and on the free movement of such data.

referenceDocuments.name: ICAO Annex 15 — Aeronautical Information Services
referenceDocuments.description: Standards and recommended practices for the provision and dissemination of aeronautical information necessary for the safety, regularity and efficiency of international air navigation.

referenceDocuments.name: AIXM and FIXM — Aviation and Flight Information Exchange Models
referenceDocuments.description: Joint reference to the Aeronautical Information Exchange Model (AIXM, EUROCONTROL/FAA) and the Flight Information Exchange Model (FIXM, ICAO/FAA), the standard data models for aeronautical information and Flight Plan data exchange.

stakeholderCategories.name: Airport

stakeholderCategories.name: Airspace User
stakeholderCategories.description: Airspace Users are entities that utilize airspace for their operations, including commercial airlines, general aviation, military, and other operators

stakeholderCategories.name: CFSP
stakeholderCategories.description: Computerized Flight Service Provider - provides flight planning and related services on behalf of airspace users

stakeholderCategories.name: National / European Authority
stakeholderCategories.description: National or European Aviation Authority / Civil Aviation Authority (CAA)
